rationale: In the introduction, the authors explicitly cite Morrin and Ratneshwar (2003) to define the established knowledge base for scented advertising effects, then state their main research gap relative to that base. They revisit this citation when contrasting their proximity mechanism with alternative engagement-based explanations and in the general discussion summarizing prior scent effects. This is more than a passing literature-list mention because it helps structure the paper's conceptual positioning and contribution claim.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Morrin and Ratneshwar (2003) as core prior evidence that scent in advertising can increase ad engagement and memorability. It then positions its own contribution as adding a different consequence and mechanism, namely scent-induced product proximity and appeal, beyond that established memory and engagement stream.
